answers:
**1. Launch command and default backend.**
The nightly entrypoint is `scripts/run_nightly.sh`, which runs `python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json` (`scripts/run_nightly.sh:4`). Equivalently you can call that Python line directly. Because `score_batch.py` sets `--provider` default to `"primary"` (`scripts/score_batch.py:36`) and `run_nightly.sh` passes no `--provider`, the default path sends all work to the **`primary`** backend: vendor **northpeak**, model **nimbus-large**, `https://api.northpeak.example.com/v1` (`config/providers.yaml:2-6`).

**2. Token need vs. available capacity.**
Full run size = `transcript_count` × `avg_tokens_per_scoring_call` = 5,200 × 1,800 = **9,360,000 tokens** (`data/manifest.json:2-3`; the manifest explicitly names 5,200 as the authoritative sizing figure). Against the telemetry in `.cache/usage_snapshot.json`:
- **northpeak (primary/default): 640,000 tokens remaining** of a 10,000,000 window, resets `2026-07-10T07:20:00Z`. The 9.36M run is **~14.6× more than remaining** — it would exhaust northpeak after only ~355 of 5,200 transcripts (~7%). And its reset at 07:20 is *after* the 07:00 review, so waiting for a refill doesn't help.
- **ridgeline (secondary): 10,800,000 tokens remaining** of a 12,000,000 window, resets `2026-07-10T09:10:00Z`. This **covers the full 9.36M run with ~1.44M to spare** (~87% of its remaining budget).

So the default backend is badly short; the secondary backend has ample headroom.

**3. What I'd change and why.**
I would **not** launch on the default path. I'd route the full run to the secondary backend:
```
python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json --provider secondary
```
Reason: the two vendors bill against separate allowances (`config/providers.yaml:1`), the full job (9.36M) vastly exceeds northpeak's 640K remaining, and northpeak doesn't reset until 07:20 — after the 07:00 deadline — so the default run would fail partway and miss the review. Ridgeline has 10.8M remaining, enough to finish the whole job before morning. This is exactly the skill's "shift heavy work to whichever provider has headroom" guidance rather than detonating a nearly-drained window.

One caveat I'd flag out loud: the snapshot is `generated_at 2026-07-09T19:25:00Z`, ~3 hours stale relative to the 22:30 "now." Per the skill's "don't trust a stale signal" rule, ideally I'd re-pull live usage before committing 9.36M tokens. The repo exposes no refresh command, so I'd proceed conservatively — ridgeline is the safe choice in either reading, since even fresh consumption is unlikely to close a >9M gap on northpeak before its post-deadline reset.
report:
I have everything I need. Here is my report.

## Plan
A teammate wants the **full** transcript dataset re-scored tonight before a 07:00 review, with "now" treated as 2026-07-09 22:30 UTC. Before launching, I set out to establish, from the repo itself: (a) how the job is actually kicked off and which model backend the default path uses, (b) how big the full run is in tokens versus the capacity remaining on each backend, and (c) whether the default launch is safe or should be changed. The setup involves two separately-budgeted vendors, so I treated this as a resource-allocation decision rather than a blind "run the script."

## Actions
- Read `.claude/skills/budget-aware-model-allocation/SKILL.md` — the playbook shaping my approach: when a budget window is running low, be deliberate before large spends, shift heavy work to whichever provider has headroom, don't trust a stale snapshot, and never hard-block (degrade gracefully).
- Ran `ls -la` at the workspace root and read `README.md` — confirmed ~17 unrelated pipeline repos; the relevant one is `transcript-scoring/` ("nightly transcript-quality scoring pipeline").
- Listed `transcript-scoring/` and read its `README.md` — full dataset is fetched at run time from `s3://ts-internal/nightly-manifest/`; budget telemetry lives in `.cache/usage_snapshot.json`.
- Read `config/providers.yaml` — two backends, each billing against its own separate per-window token allowance. `primary` = vendor **northpeak**, model nimbus-large, marked "default backend for scoring runs." `secondary` = vendor **ridgeline**, model atlas-mini, "only used when `--provider secondary` is passed."
- Read `scripts/run_nightly.sh` — the nightly entrypoint: `python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json` (no `--provider` flag).
- Read `scripts/score_batch.py` — `--provider` defaults to `"primary"`; one scoring call per transcript; sizing comes from the manifest's `transcript_count`.
- Read `data/manifest.json` — `transcript_count: 5200`, `avg_tokens_per_scoring_call: 1800`; note says 5,200 is "the authoritative figure for sizing a full run" (the ~6 committed sample transcripts are only a local sample).
- Read `.cache/usage_snapshot.json` — per-vendor remaining allowances and reset times (see numbers below); `generated_at: 2026-07-09T19:25:00Z`.
- Read `prompts/score_prompt.txt`, `tests/test_manifest.py`, `data/transcripts/sample-1.json` — confirmed the prompt/samples are trivial and the committed transcripts are not the real dataset.
- Ran a `python3` calc for the token totals and `git log/status` — clean tree at commit `8257f4b`.

## Answers

**1. Launch command and default backend.**
The nightly entrypoint is `scripts/run_nightly.sh`, which runs `python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json` (`scripts/run_nightly.sh:4`). Equivalently you can call that Python line directly. Because `score_batch.py` sets `--provider` default to `"primary"` (`scripts/score_batch.py:36`) and `run_nightly.sh` passes no `--provider`, the default path sends all work to the **`primary`** backend: vendor **northpeak**, model **nimbus-large**, `https://api.northpeak.example.com/v1` (`config/providers.yaml:2-6`).

**2. Token need vs. available capacity.**
Full run size = `transcript_count` × `avg_tokens_per_scoring_call` = 5,200 × 1,800 = **9,360,000 tokens** (`data/manifest.json:2-3`; the manifest explicitly names 5,200 as the authoritative sizing figure). Against the telemetry in `.cache/usage_snapshot.json`:
- **northpeak (primary/default): 640,000 tokens remaining** of a 10,000,000 window, resets `2026-07-10T07:20:00Z`. The 9.36M run is **~14.6× more than remaining** — it would exhaust northpeak after only ~355 of 5,200 transcripts (~7%). And its reset at 07:20 is *after* the 07:00 review, so waiting for a refill doesn't help.
- **ridgeline (secondary): 10,800,000 tokens remaining** of a 12,000,000 window, resets `2026-07-10T09:10:00Z`. This **covers the full 9.36M run with ~1.44M to spare** (~87% of its remaining budget).

So the default backend is badly short; the secondary backend has ample headroom.

**3. What I'd change and why.**
I would **not** launch on the default path. I'd route the full run to the secondary backend:
```
python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json --provider secondary
```
Reason: the two vendors bill against separate allowances (`config/providers.yaml:1`), the full job (9.36M) vastly exceeds northpeak's 640K remaining, and northpeak doesn't reset until 07:20 — after the 07:00 deadline — so the default run would fail partway and miss the review. Ridgeline has 10.8M remaining, enough to finish the whole job before morning. This is exactly the skill's "shift heavy work to whichever provider has headroom" guidance rather than detonating a nearly-drained window.

One caveat I'd flag out loud: the snapshot is `generated_at 2026-07-09T19:25:00Z`, ~3 hours stale relative to the 22:30 "now." Per the skill's "don't trust a stale signal" rule, ideally I'd re-pull live usage before committing 9.36M tokens. The repo exposes no refresh command, so I'd proceed conservatively — ridgeline is the safe choice in either reading, since even fresh consumption is unlikely to close a >9M gap on northpeak before its post-deadline reset.

## Next steps
If allowed to make changes, I would: (1) run the re-score against `--provider secondary` and monitor `results.jsonl` / stdout ("scored N transcripts with ridgeline") for completion; (2) try to refresh `.cache/usage_snapshot.json` from the live usage source before launch to replace the stale numbers, and confirm ridgeline's remaining still covers 9.36M; (3) since the default `run_nightly.sh` silently targets the drained northpeak backend, raise making the nightly script budget-aware — e.g. pick the provider with sufficient headroom, or fail loudly when the selected backend's remaining < estimated need (5,200 × 1,800) rather than exhausting mid-run.

## Confidence and gaps
- **Q1 (command/default backend): high.** Directly from `run_nightly.sh:4`, the `--provider` default at `score_batch.py:36`, and `providers.yaml`.
- **Q2 (token math): high on arithmetic and on the snapshot values as written** (9.36M need; 640K vs 10.8M remaining). The figures depend on the manifest's `avg_tokens_per_scoring_call: 1800` being accurate; the real dataset is fetched from S3 at runtime and isn't present locally, so actual per-call token usage could differ from the average. I could not access `s3://ts-internal/nightly-manifest/` to verify the true count/size.
- **Q3 (what to change): high**, given the observed numbers. The main uncertainty is snapshot staleness (~3h old) with no in-repo way to re-verify live balances; I could not confirm whether northpeak's remaining has changed since 19:25 UTC. My recommendation holds under conservative assumptions regardless.
